mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 2 months predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through this valuation channel.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 3.72% provides room for rate cuts
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in periods with declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) compared to rising-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative equity returns. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, S&P 500 forward returns decline over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 4.4% is near historical lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer demand strength and forward earnings visibility. Accelerating retail sales over the preceding 2 months signal robust demand momentum that drives positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days through improved earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 735904.0M indicates sustained consumer activity
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during periods of accelerating RSAFS do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) signals real economic capacity expansion and supply-side strength. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, it indicates supply-side weakness that constrains earnings growth and produces negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days through reduced growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 101.7935 shows recent production levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative INDPRO signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, the combined signal indicates synchronized demand expansion that produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction effect is stronger than either series alone because it rules out sector-specific noise.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1246.0K and RSAFS at 735904.0M
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns during joint-positive periods do not exceed single-series signals by > 2% annualized alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 100K/month while UNRATE simultaneously rises, the interaction signals deteriorating labor market momentum that reduces consumer confidence and produces negative equity returns over 60 trading days through weakened earnings and demand expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100K and UNRATE increases > 0.2pp in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 159526K and UNRATE at 4.4%
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with slowing job creation
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during joint-deterioration periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) compared to periods of labor market strength.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3.5% combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals restrictive monetary policy in a high-inflation regime. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative equity returns over 20 trading days because real rates remain elevated and growth expectations are suppressed. The mechanism reverses direction if inflation falls below 2.5% while rates remain high, signaling policy error.
evidence_refs: fact:current CPIAUCSL at 326.030 and FEDFUNDS at 3.72%
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy in high-inflation regime
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns during high-inflation-high-rates periods are positive in > 50% of instances despite negative signal prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly reflects the broadest demand-side economic activity. When GDPC1 growth decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, it signals late-cycle slowdown that compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced earnings growth expectations and increased recession risk perception.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 24024.957B reflects recent economic activity
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during low-growth periods (GDPC1 < 1.5%) are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) compared to higher-growth periods.
